name: Lemon Meringue Angel Food Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces unsalted butter (6 tablespoons), chilled and cut into 6 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place fresh, cold egg whites and cold water in the bowl of a heavy-duty mixer. Set aside until they reach 60 degrees or slightly below room temperature.
2. Pour flour, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, and salt into a triple sifter or sieve. Sift onto a sheet of waxed paper; set aside.
3. Adjust rack to the lower third of the oven and preheat to 325 degrees Fahrenheit. Set nearby an ungreased 10-inch tube pan. This type of pan allows the batter to cling to the sides and rise to its full height.
4. Place a long-necked bottle or large metal funnel nearby for inverting the baked cake.
5. Whip egg whites, preferably with a whisk attachment, until frothy. Add cream of tartar and whip on medium speed until soft peaks form. Continue whipping and gradually add the 1 cup granulated sugar until whites have thickened and form soft droopy white peaks. Add vanilla and lemon zest in the final moments of whipping.
6. Sprinkle one-quarter of the flour mixture over the whites, and with a rubber spatula, fold into the whites. Repeat this process with the remaining flour mixture, folding in only one-quarter at a time. Gently pour batter into an ungreased tube pan.
7. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until the top is light golden, the cake feels spongy and springs back when touched, and a wooden toothpick inserted in the middle comes out free of cake. Invert the cake onto the neck of a bottle or funnel. Cool completely before removing from pan.
8. To remove the cake from the pan, slip just the tip of a small metal spatula between the cake and pan. Slowly trace the perimeter to release any cake sticking to the pan. Tilt the cake pan on its side and gently tap the bottom against the counter to loosen the cake. Rotate the cake pan, tapping as you turn it a few more times until it appears free. Cover the cake with a rack or cardboard round and tap firmly onto the surface. Lift the pan from the cake.
9. For the filling: In a 1 1/2 quart heavy-bottomed saucepan, whisk eggs, egg yolks, sugar, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Add butter pieces and place over medium heat, stirring constantly until the entire bottom is cooked. Cook without boiling until it begins to develop body and thicken. Remove from heat and pour through a stainless steel sieve into a bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and poke small slits for steam to escape while cooling. Refrigerate (curd will thicken).
10. For the frosting: In a 3-cup saucepan, combine water and 2/3 cup sugar. Place over low heat to dissolve the sugar, then increase heat to medium-high and boil without stirring until a candy thermometer reads 235 degrees Fahrenheit. Wash down any sugar crystals with a brush dipped in cold water. Near the end of boiling time, whip egg whites with cream of tartar until soft peaks form. Add 2 tablespoons sugar and continue whipping until stiff but not dry. With mixer running, pour syrup onto whipped whites. Continue whipping on medium speed for about 3 more minutes. Mixture will thicken, cool, and form glossy, stiff peaks. Add vanilla. Cool to room temperature, then frost cake right away.
11. Assemble the dessert by cutting the cake into three layers. Spread the lemon filling equally between the two layers; top with the remaining layer. Frost the top and sides of the cake with Italian Meringue Frosting.
